page: Microsoft Azure E16a_v4 is an EAv4-series virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7452 processor, operating at a clock speed of 3.35 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 16 MB of L3 cache. It features 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 410 GB, and there are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates balanced mid-range performance across most workloads. The server achieves average multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 6102.29 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 8135.0. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with the stress-ng single-core test ranking in the bottom 10% at 768.29. Memory bandwidth is stable, reaching 795.44 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 52.1 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. In database and key-value store tests, the instance processes 14.24 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 5090.3 in PassMark database operations, both falling within the average tier.

Given the 128.0 GB memory capacity and average multi-threaded processing capabilities, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that do not require peak single-core performance. It is well-suited for relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, and moderate data processing tasks. Conversely, it is less optimal for heavy single-threaded execution or larger artificial intelligence tasks, as demonstrated by weak performance in medium 7B LLM inference tests.
description: Microsoft Azure E16a_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor running at 3.35 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This x86_64 server includes 410 GB of local storage but lacks complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier for multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core performance represents a clear tradeoff, scoring in the bottom 10% on stress-ng tests. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, the E16a_v4 offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads. It is best suited for database hosting, in-memory caching, and general-purpose enterprise applications that require substantial memory capacity without demanding extreme single-threaded CPU performance.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16a_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and 410 GB of local storage, delivering balanced multi-core performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16a_v4 is an x86_64 virtual machine with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 410 GB local storage, optimized for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and local storage for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores
Equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory and 410 GB of local storage
Delivers average multi-core performance and memory bandwidth across standard benchmarks
Exhibits lower single-core CPU performance as a primary hardware tradeoff
Optimized for memory-intensive workloads, database hosting, and in-memory caching
